name: Copper sensor (ACE1 + CRISPRa)
shortDescription: Detects copper in water using the native yeast copper-responsive activator ACE1 and its CUP1 promoter, gating a CRISPRa circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in GRAS baker's yeast.

sensing.sensorMechanism: Copper-bound ACE1 activates the CUP1 promoter (PCUP1).
sensing.signalFlow: Cu -> ACE1 activates PCUP1 -> sgRNA -> dCas9-VPR amplifies the reporter (CRISPRa) -> fluorescence rises with copper.

parts.name: sgRNA
parts.source: designed against / upstream of the reporter promoter

performance.notes: ACE1/CUP1 is a classic copper-responsive yeast system; integrated CRISPRa device is a design template.

buildSteps.title: Design sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target/position the sgRNA; check S. cerevisiae off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: Sensor -> sgRNA; dCas9; reporter. Use the host's standard toolkit.

buildSteps.title: Transform S. cerevisiae
buildSteps.description: Transform, select, and confirm low background.

buildSteps.title: Expose to sample
buildSteps.description: Incubate with the sample plus a copper standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the native yeast ACE1/CUP1 copper sensor with CRISPRa
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
